title: bauhinia rufa var. dodecandra
intro: Bauhinia rufa var. dodecandra, also known as the Soapbark Tree, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, specifically in the tropical regions of East and Central Africa. The medicinal properties of Bauhinia rufa var. dodecandra include its ability to inhibit the growth of certain microorganisms, making it a potential antiseptic and antibacterial agent. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. The herbal preparations of Bauhinia rufa var. dodecandra typically involve the bark, which can be dried and crushed to make a tea or infused in hot water to create a decoction, often used to treat various ailments.
